title: Peppery Crispy Kenyan Haddock Skewers
description: A low-carb, crispy, and peppery Kenyan-inspired dish featuring haddock, perfect for a midnight craving. The fermentation process adds a unique twist to this flavorful snack.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together grated ginger, minced garlic, crushed kenyan bird's eye chilies, and coarsely ground black peppercorns.
Add the haddock pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
In a separate bowl, mix together lemon juice and coconut oil.
Pour the lemon juice mixture over the haddock pieces and toss to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Thread the marinated haddock pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Dip each skewer into the almond flour to coat, shaking off excess.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 12-15 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the haddock is cooking, heat a tablespoon of coconut oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped fermented cabbage to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until slightly caramelized.
Serve the haddock skewers with the caramelized fermented cabbage and a sprinkle of salt to taste.
